sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 13.40 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.13)

statement.latex: A group$G$is said to be$$-resi\-du\-al\-ly free\/ if, for every finite set of non-trivial elements of$G$, there is a homomorphism of~$G$into a free group such that the images of all these elements are non-trivial. Is every finitely-generated$$-resi\-du\-al\-ly free group embeddable in a free${ Z} [x]$-group? { A.\,G.\,Myasnikov,~V.\,N.\,Remeslennikov} Yes, it is (O.\,Kharlampovich, A.\,Myasnikov, ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
